thi9:1.0.204: “Vaḍḍha, please never everget entangled in the world.My child, do not partakein suffering again and again.
thi9:1.0.205: For happy dwell the sages, Vaḍḍha,unstirred, their doubts cut off,cooled and tamed,and free of defilements.
thi9:1.0.206: Vaḍḍha, foster the paththat the hermits have walked,for the attainment of vision,and for making an end of suffering.”
thi9:1.0.207: “Mother, you speak with such assuranceto me on this matter.My dear mom, I can’t help thinkingthat no entanglements are found in you.”
thi9:1.0.208: “Vaḍḍha, not a jot or a skerrickof entanglement is found in mefor any conditions at all,whether low, high, or middling.
thi9:1.0.209: All defilements are ended for me,meditating and diligent.I’ve attained the three knowledgesand fulfilled the Buddha’s instructions.”
thi9:1.0.210: “Oh so excellent was the goadmy mother spurred me with!Owing to her compassion, she spokeverses on the ultimate goal.
thi9:1.0.211: On hearing her words,advised by my mother,I was struck with righteous urgencyfor the sake of finding sanctuary.
thi9:1.0.212: Striving, resolute,tireless all day and night,urged on by my mother,I realized supreme peace.
